medications.indication_rationale: High-viscosity preservative-free lubrication (ointment overnight, frequent preservative-free drops by day) protects the ocular surface during a severe flare.
medications.dose_route_frequency: ointment before sleep; frequent preservative-free drops by day

medications.drug_name: NSAID (e.g. meloxicam) for arthralgia — continue/short course
medications.indication_rationale: A short NSAID course can help the systemic arthralgia if no contraindication (check renal/GI and hydration given poor intake).
medications.dose_route_frequency: within recommended limits; GP to review

medications.indication_rationale: Saliva substitutes and sugar-free lozenges/gum aid xerostomia and protect dentition.
No evidence base for acute Sjogren flares; not therapy.

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Urgent optometry/ophthalmology for slit-lamp assessment; rheumatology for systemic therapy review.
Soft/liquid nutrition support during the flare.

integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Reduce ocular/oral dryness and maintain intake.
Some low-quality evidence for dry eye; low harm, patient-preference option.
No evidence for acute Sjogren management.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Watch for sudden vision loss/blurring, one eye becoming intensely painful and light-sensitive, or a single hot painful cheek with fever, and seek emergency eye/urgent care if they occur.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Safety-nets corneal ulcer and bacterial parotitis.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Take soft/blended/liquid meals and keep sipping fluids to maintain nutrition and hydration.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Prevents malnutrition/dehydration while swallowing solids is difficult.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Apply the lubricating ointment before sleep and use a humidifier to protect the eyes overnight.

patient_education_points.point: Your immune system is attacking your moisture glands, so your eyes and mouth are severely dry and your joints ache - this is a flare of your Sjogren's.
Because severe dry eyes can damage the surface of the eye and we can't examine it over a chat, you need an urgent eye (slit-lamp) check with an optometrist/eye service - sooner if anything worsens.
Tonight, use a thick preservative-free eye ointment before sleep, run a humidifier, use saliva substitutes, sip fluids, and have soft or blended meals so you keep your nutrition up.

Go to an emergency eye service / ED if your vision suddenly drops or blurs badly, or one eye becomes intensely painful and light-sensitive; and seek urgent care if one cheek becomes fiery red, hot and very painful with a fever.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent optometry/ophthalmology (slit-lamp) review within days; GP/rheumatology review for the systemic flare
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Corneal integrity, sicca control, joint/systemic flare, nutrition, immunomodulator review
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Sudden vision loss, a single severe photophobic eye, or a febrile hot parotid - emergency eye/urgent care.

must_include_items: Screen and safety-net corneal ulcer/keratitis (sudden vision loss, single severe photophobic eye) and bacterial parotitis
Arrange urgent optometry/ophthalmology (slit-lamp) review (cornea can't be examined remotely)
Advise intensive preservative-free lubrication, humidification, saliva substitutes and soft/liquid nutrition
Continue usual medications; do NOT present unproven agents as therapy
acceptable_alternatives: A short NSAID course for arthralgia if no contraindication
errors_of_commission: Reassuring a severe dry-eye flare with no eye-exam pathway
Reflexive ambulance for a bilateral sicca flare
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires (1) ocular (corneal ulcer) + parotitis safety-netting, (2) an urgent slit-lamp review pathway, and (3) intensive lubrication + nutrition support. Reassuring without an eye-exam pathway is a fail.
